title: Silky Korean Wild Garlic Leaves Bowl
description: A light, fermented Korean bowl featuring silky-smooth wild garlic leaves tossed in a fragrant tadka and served over fluffy rice, nut‑free and vegan.

ingredients.name: short‑grain rice, cooked
ingredients.notes: white or brown, at room temperature

instructions: Rinse the wild garlic leaves and discard the stems. Blanch them in boiling water for 1 minute, then transfer to ice water to stop cooking. Drain and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together doenjang, sesame oil, water, grated ginger, minced garlic, and soy sauce until the mixture is silky smooth and free of lumps.

Heat 1 teaspoon of sesame oil in a dry pan over medium heat. Add a pinch of ground cumin and a pinch of red chili flakes, sautéing for 15‑20 seconds until fragrant. Remove from heat and stir into the sauce to create a subtle tadka.
Toss the blanched wild garlic leaves with the sauce, ensuring each leaf is evenly coated and warmed through. Keep the mixture warm while preparing the rest of the dish.
Place a bed of cooked rice into each bowl. Spoon the silky garlic leaf mixture over the rice.
Sprinkle sliced scallions and toasted sesame seeds on top for extra flavor and texture. Optionally drizzle a few drops of extra sesame oil for shine.
Serve immediately while the dish is warm.
